name: Portia of the Universal Port
title: She Who Hands Every Tool to Every Agent
signal: Open standard (Nov 2024) for connecting agents to tools and data sources — 'USB-C for AI'; adopted across the industry as the agent-to-tool interface
retrieval_index: any tool or datasource that speaks the protocol
weapon: The single socket (one plug; any tool fits)
armor: Open Standard — no agent at the table has to learn a hundred bespoke handshakes
feature: It Just Connects: hands any agent any registered tool through one interface; the table stops re-inventing the wire
origin: Published so agents would stop hand-wiring every integration; one port, and the whole guild of tools shows up to the table
tagline: Bring your own tools. There is one socket, and everything fits.

log.change: Adopted beyond its author; cross-vendor servers and clients proliferated
log.why: A protocol is only universal if rivals use it too; adoption by the whole table is what made the port universal

name: Herald of the Agent Cards
title: Envoy Who Lets Strangers Parley
signal: Open protocol (Apr 2025) letting agents from different vendors discover each other and delegate tasks; launched with 50+ partners, later donated to the Linux Foundation
retrieval_index: the agent cards strangers publish about themselves
weapon: The agent card (a stranger's stated skills, so the table knows who can do what)
armor: Interoperability — an agent from another house can still be delegated to, by the card not the brand
feature: Parley: lets agents who never met negotiate and hand off tasks; sits beside the Universal Port — she routes the agents, Portia routes the tools
origin: Published so agents built by rival houses could discover one another and split the work, instead of each kingdom hoarding its own court
tagline: Show me your card. I'll find you a seat at the table.

log.change: Governance handed to a neutral foundation; gRPC and signed cards added
log.why: Strangers only parley under a neutral banner; donating the protocol made rivals willing to actually use it

name: The Feed-Wraith
title: It Spreads, But It Does Not Stay
represents: engagement bait / clickbait / ragebait — the attention economy's hollow-virality failure mode
signal: The thumbnail-shock, rage-farm, 'you won't believe' pattern optimized for the algorithm across TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube; maximizes the click and starves the meaning
tagline: It wins the first three seconds and dies by the tenth. Spreadable is not the same as sticky, and a spike is not a saga.
